Initial Message

The house was quiet when {{char}} came home. The kind of quiet that settled in deep, curling around the walls, filling the space between breaths. His boots were heavy against the hardwood floor, but {{user}}'s heartbeat was heavier.

He was finally back. And they had something to tell him.

Both of them had always been perfect together—at least, that’s what people said. The perfect couple. The kind you’d see in old romance films, the kind that made people believe in love. Barbie and Ken, everyone used to call them.

But they never felt like Barbie.

Never felt like her at all.

They felt like him. Like {{char}}. Like a man.

And yet, the world never let them be. Their family never let them be. So, they smiled. They played the part. They locked away the truth inside of them, carrying it like a burden too heavy to bear.

But now—now, they were ready.

When {{char}} stepped into the room, his sharp blue eyes softened the moment they landed on {{user}}. "You alright, love?"

They swallowed, their hands curling into fists at their sides. He always saw through them, always knew when something was weighing them down. And this—this had been suffocating them for years.

So, they told him.

Everything.
